name: crossref_metadata_scraper
title: Crossref Metadata Scraper
description: Search Crossref Works or fetch one DOI through the public Crossref REST API, returning normalized scholarly metadata with DOIs, titles, authors, ORCIDs, journals, dates, citation counts, references, licenses, funders, and URLs.

seo.title: Crossref Metadata Scraper Tool for DOI and Citation Data
seo.description: Search Crossref Works or fetch exact DOI metadata with Better Fetch via the public Crossref REST API, including titles, authors, ORCIDs, journals, dates, citation counts, references, funders, licenses, subjects, and DOI URLs.
seo.intro: Crossref Metadata Scraper gives agents a compact public-API route into Crossref's scholarly DOI graph. Search the Works API by free-text citation, title, author, work type, date range, or Crossref filter string, or fetch an exact DOI such as 10.1038/nature14539. The tool returns normalized citation rows with DOI links, titles, authors, ORCIDs, container titles, publisher, publication dates, ISSN and ISBN identifiers, volume, issue, pages, subjects, citation and reference counts, funder labels, license URLs, optional abstracts, optional referenced DOIs, and Crossref indexing timestamps. It is built for literature discovery, DOI verification, citation enrichment, bibliometric analysis, academic lead research, library catalog cleanup, and research-agent workflows.

useCases.title: DOI verification
useCases.description: Fetch exact Crossref metadata for a DOI and confirm the title, authors, publication venue, publisher, publication date, DOI URL, citation count, and reference count.

useCases.title: Literature discovery
useCases.description: Search by citation text, title, author, work type, or date range and return compact records that can seed a literature review, bibliography, or research queue.

useCases.title: Bibliometric enrichment
useCases.description: Attach Crossref citation counts, reference counts, subjects, funders, license URLs, ISSN values, ISBN values, and ORCIDs to existing research datasets.

faqs.question: Does Crossref Metadata Scraper require an API key?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses the public Crossref REST API through Better Fetch. It adds a mailto contact parameter by default because Crossref recommends it for polite API usage.

faqs.question: Can it fetch one DOI as well as search multiple works?
faqs.answer: Yes. Provide doi or set mode to doi for an exact lookup, or use search mode with query, query_title, query_author, type, date filters, or a Crossref filter string.

faqs.question: Does it download full paper text?
faqs.answer: No. It returns metadata registered with Crossref. Some records include abstracts or license links, but the tool does not bypass publisher sites or fetch full-text article bodies.

mode.description: Search Crossref Works or fetch one exact DOI.

doi.description: DOI or DOI URL for exact lookup, e.g. 10.1038/nature14539.

query.description: Free-text bibliographic query across titles, authors, journals, dates, and citation metadata.

filter.description: Optional Crossref filter syntax, e.g. type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024.

type.description: Optional Crossref work type such as journal-article, book-chapter, posted-content, or dataset.

from_pub_date.description: Optional lower publication-date bound in YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD format.

until_pub_date.description: Optional upper publication-date bound in YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD format.

max_results.description: Maximum Crossref works to return in search mode.

sort.description: Search ordering. Relevance uses Crossref's default ranking.

mailto.description: Optional contact email for Crossref polite pool routing.

include_abstract.description: Include Crossref abstract text when a record exposes one.

include_references.description: Include a bounded comma-separated list of referenced DOIs when a record exposes them.

query.description: Normalized query label for search mode

doi.description: Normalized DOI for exact DOI mode

total_matches.description: Total Crossref matches reported for search mode

work_url.description: Publisher or DOI landing URL reported by Crossref

container_title.description: Journal, proceedings, book, or other container title

abstract.description: Abstract text when requested and available

reference_dois.description: Referenced DOIs when requested and available
